title: 1251. What is the capital of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands?
hint: It is named after a British naval officer.
summary: Port Blair is the capital of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.

title: 1252. The Cellular Jail, also known as 'Kala Pani', is located in which city?
hint: It is the capital of the Union Territory.
summary: The Cellular Jail is located in Port Blair.

title: 1253. Which is the southernmost point of the territory of India?
hint: It lies in Great Nicobar Island.
summary: Indira Point is the southernmost point of the territory of India.

title: 1254. Indira Point is located on which island?
hint: It is the largest island in the Nicobar group.
summary: Indira Point is situated on Great Nicobar Island.

title: 1255. The Andaman and Nicobar Islands are separated by which channel?
hint: The channel is named after a British naval officer.
summary: The Ten Degree Channel separates the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.

title: 1256. Which is the only active volcano in India?
hint: It is located in the Andaman Sea.
summary: Barren Island is home to India's only active volcano.

title: 1257. Narcondam Island is famous for which endangered bird?
hint: It is found only on this island.
summary: Narcondam Island is home to the endemic Narcondam Hornbill.

title: 1258. Which sea surrounds the Andaman and Nicobar Islands?
hint: It lies east of the Bay of Bengal.
summary: The islands are located in the Andaman Sea and the Bay of Bengal.

title: 1259. Which island group is located north of the Ten Degree Channel?
hint: It shares its name with the Union Territory.
summary: The Andaman Islands lie north of the Ten Degree Channel.

title: 1260. Which island group lies south of the Ten Degree Channel?
hint: It contains Great Nicobar Island.
summary: The Nicobar Islands lie south of the Ten Degree Channel.

title: 1261. Which is the largest island in the Andaman group?
hint: It is the northernmost major island of the Andaman group.
summary: North Andaman Island is the largest island in the Andaman group.

title: 1262. Saddle Peak, the highest point in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, is located on which island?
hint: It is also the largest island in the Andaman group.
summary: Saddle Peak is located in North Andaman Island.

title: 1263. Which national park is famous for protecting marine ecosystems in the Andaman Islands?
hint: It is located near Wandoor.
summary: Mahatma Gandhi Marine National Park conserves coral reefs and marine biodiversity.

title: 1264. Which indigenous tribe is known for living on North Sentinel Island?
hint: The tribe has minimal contact with the outside world.
summary: The Sentinelese inhabit North Sentinel Island.

title: 1265. Which tribe mainly inhabits Little Andaman Island?
hint: They are among the Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs).
summary: The Onge tribe mainly inhabits Little Andaman Island.

title: 1266. Which tribe primarily lives in the forests of Great Nicobar Island?
hint: This tribe is a PVTG.
summary: The Shompen tribe primarily inhabits Great Nicobar Island.

title: 1267. Which tribe forms the largest indigenous community in the Nicobar Islands?
hint: They are mainly engaged in coconut cultivation.
summary: The Nicobarese are the largest indigenous community in the Nicobar Islands.

title: 1268. Which biosphere reserve is located in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands?
hint: It includes Campbell Bay and Galathea National Parks.
summary: Great Nicobar Biosphere Reserve is a UNESCO-recognized biosphere reserve.

title: 1269. Which strait separates Little Andaman from South Andaman?
hint: It is named after a British naval officer.
summary: Duncan Passage separates Little Andaman from South Andaman.

title: 1270. Which ocean surrounds the Andaman and Nicobar Islands?
hint: It is the third-largest ocean in the world.
summary: The Andaman and Nicobar Islands are located in the Indian Ocean region.

title: 1271. Which island is the headquarters of the Nicobar district?
hint: It is one of the northernmost islands in the Nicobar group.
summary: Car Nicobar is the headquarters of the Nicobar district.

title: 1272. Which is the only inhabited volcanic island in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands?
hint: It is famous for an endemic hornbill.
summary: Narcondam Island is an inhabited volcanic island.

title: 1273. Which national park is located in Great Nicobar Island?
hint: It shares its name with the southernmost bay of India.
summary: Galathea National Park is located on Great Nicobar Island.

title: 1274. Which island is famous for Radhanagar Beach?
hint: It was renamed after a freedom fighter in 2018.
summary: Radhanagar Beach is located on Swaraj Dweep (formerly Havelock Island).

title: 1275. Shaheed Dweep was formerly known by which name?
hint: It was renamed in December 2018.
summary: Shaheed Dweep was formerly known as Neil Island.

title: 1276. Swaraj Dweep was formerly known by which name?
hint: It was renamed in 2018.
summary: Swaraj Dweep was formerly known as Havelock Island.

title: 1277. Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose Island was formerly known as:
hint: It served as the British administrative headquarters before Independence.
summary: Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose Island was formerly known as Ross Island.

title: 1278. Which tribe is primarily found in the Middle and South Andaman Islands?
hint: The Andaman Trunk Road passes through parts of their reserve.
summary: The Jarwa tribe inhabits parts of Middle and South Andaman Islands.

title: 1279. Which is the largest island in the Nicobar group?
hint: It contains India's southernmost point.
summary: Great Nicobar is the largest island in the Nicobar group.

title: 1280. Which island is the location of Campbell Bay?
hint: It is India's southernmost large island.
summary: Campbell Bay is located on Great Nicobar Island.

title: 1281. Which Indian Prime Minister officially renamed Havelock Island as Swaraj Dweep in 2018?
hint: The renaming was announced on the 75th anniversary of Netaji's flag hoisting.
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi renamed Havelock Island as Swaraj Dweep in 2018.

title: 1282. Which freedom fighter first hoisted the Indian National Flag in Port Blair during World War II?
hint: He was the leader of the Azad Hind Government.
summary: Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose hoisted the Indian National Flag at Port Blair on 30 December 1943.

title: 1283. The Andaman and Nicobar Command is India's first:
hint: It combines the Army, Navy and Air Force under one command.
summary: The Andaman and Nicobar Command is India's first tri-service theatre command.

title: 1284. The Andaman and Nicobar Islands are separated from Myanmar by which channel?
hint: It lies north of North Andaman Island.
summary: Preparis Channel separates the Andaman Islands from Myanmar.

title: 1285. Which biosphere reserve of India is located entirely on an island?
hint: It is located in the southernmost Union Territory.
summary: Great Nicobar Biosphere Reserve is India's island biosphere reserve.

title: 1286. Which is the principal language spoken by the Nicobarese tribe?
hint: It belongs to the Austroasiatic language family.
summary: Nicobarese people primarily speak Nicobarese languages.

title: 1287. Which lighthouse marks the southernmost tip of India?
hint: It was damaged during the 2004 tsunami.
summary: The Indira Point Lighthouse marks India's southernmost point.

title: 1288. Which major natural disaster significantly altered the coastline of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands in 2004?
hint: It followed one of the strongest earthquakes ever recorded.
summary: The 2004 Indian Ocean tsunami significantly altered the islands' coastline.

title: 1289. Which crop is the major plantation crop of the Nicobar Islands?
hint: It is widely used for producing copra.
summary: Coconut is the major plantation crop of the Nicobar Islands.

title: 1290. The Andaman and Nicobar Islands fall under which High Court?
hint: Its principal seat is in Kolkata.
summary: The Andaman and Nicobar Islands come under the jurisdiction of the Calcutta High Court.

title: 1291. Which island is famous for the natural limestone caves near Baratang?
hint: It lies between South and North Andaman.
summary: The limestone caves are located on Baratang Island.

title: 1292. Which active mud volcano of India is located in the Andaman Islands?
hint: It is a popular tourist attraction.
summary: India's only active mud volcanoes are found on Baratang Island.

title: 1293. Which island is known for Elephant Beach?
hint: It was renamed in 2018.
summary: Elephant Beach is located on Swaraj Dweep.

title: 1294. The Jarwa Tribal Reserve is mainly located in which island group?
hint: It includes Middle and South Andaman.
summary: The Jarwa Tribal Reserve spans Middle and South Andaman Islands.

title: 1295. Which sea lies immediately east of the Andaman Islands?
hint: It shares its name with the island group.
summary: The Andaman Sea lies east of the Andaman Islands.

title: 1296. Which airport serves as the main airport of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands?
hint: It was renamed after a freedom fighter.
summary: Veer Savarkar International Airport is located at Port Blair.

title: 1297. Which island hosts the headquarters of the Andaman and Nicobar Command?
hint: It is the capital island.
summary: The headquarters of the Andaman and Nicobar Command is at Port Blair in South Andaman.

title: 1298. Which marine animal is especially protected in the Mahatma Gandhi Marine National Park?
hint: It is the state animal of Andaman & Nicobar.
summary: The Dugong is an important protected marine mammal found in the park.

title: 1299. Which island is home to Campbell Bay National Park?
hint: It is India's southernmost large island.
summary: Campbell Bay National Park is located on Great Nicobar Island.

title: 1300. Which Union Territory of India consists of more than 570 islands?
hint: It lies in the Bay of Bengal.
summary: The Andaman and Nicobar Islands comprise over 570 islands, islets and rocks.
